disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Near-fainting (presyncope) brought on by exertion, resolving with rest
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when the dizziness started?
Have you actually fainted or come close to it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It hit me while I was walking out to the letterbox — I went light-headed and thought I'd black out, so I sat down and after a while it passed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chest tightness occurring with exertion at the same time as the presyncope, settling with rest after about 10 minutes
trigger_question_examples: Did you have any chest pain or tightness with it?
How long did the chest tightness last?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, my chest felt tight at the same time — a squeezing feeling across the middle. It eased off after about ten minutes of sitting still.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive breathlessness on exertion developing over the preceding three months
trigger_question_examples: Have you been more short of breath than usual lately?
How far can you walk before you get puffed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Over the last three months I've been getting more and more short of breath when I walk — more than I ever used to.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms are strictly exertional and reproducibly relieved by rest, with no fluttering/racing heartbeat noticed before the dizzy spell
trigger_question_examples: Did you notice your heart racing or fluttering before you got dizzy?
Does this only happen when you exert yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No racing or fluttering that I noticed — it only ever comes on when I'm moving around and doing things, and stops when I rest.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked fear of falling/fainting has led to self-restricted mobility; frail and lives alone with intermittent family support
trigger_question_examples: How is this affecting what you can do day to day?
Is anyone with you at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've stopped moving around much because I'm scared I'll faint and fall. My son's staying over tonight but usually I'm on my own.
